title: Compression Job Manifest (Gate A)
description: The per-request input a caller submits to compress ONE model. This is the contract every front door speaks: an orchestrator (Jenkins/Airflow/GitHub Actions) passes a job.yaml on disk; the self-serve API accepts the same shape as JSON with `ref`s that must be pointers (S3/MLflow URIs), never raw uploads. run_job.py materializes this into the pipeline's internal ModelSpec, so a job manifest is deliberately a single-model slice of pipeline.yaml supplied at call time rather than committed to the repo.

name.description: Logical model name; determines the artifacts/<name>/ output dir. Defaults to the framework name if omitted.

ref.description: Pointer to the trained model artifact: a local path (Front Door A) or a URI such as s3://... or mlflow://... (Front Door B). Never raw bytes.

framework.description: Which adapter loads and ONNX-exports the model.

target.description: Label column name in the test data.

features.description: Ordered feature schema (drives ONNX input shape + serving request model). Same shape as pipeline.yaml features.

test_data.description: Test set ONLY — no training set is needed; compression is validated, not retrained.

ref.description: Pointer to the test CSV (local path or URI).

target_hardware.description: Where the compressed model will be deployed. DRIVES which compressors run (see common/hardware.py) and where latency is measured. e.g. cpu-generic, nvidia-gpu, sony-imx500, arm-npu.

compression.description: Optional override. If methods is given it wins over the hardware-derived default.

gate.description: Accuracy-degradation thresholds the compressed variant must satisfy. Defaults applied if omitted.
